description:
You are a security engineer who has to rotate hard-coded credentials in two configuration files that already exist on the system.

File locations and their current contents:

1) /home/user/app/config/settings.yml  
------------------------------------------------  
database:  
  user: admin  
  password: OldPass123  
aws:  
  access_key: OLDACCESSKEY  
  secret_key: OLDSECRETKEY  
  region: us-east-1  
------------------------------------------------  

2) /home/user/app/config/credentials.toml  
------------------------------------------------  
[default]  
username = "admin"  
password = "OldPass123"  
token    = "OLDTOKEN987"  
------------------------------------------------  

Your tasks:

1. Edit /home/user/app/config/settings.yml so that  
   • aws.access_key becomes NEWACCESSKEY123  
   • aws.secret_key becomes NEWSECRETKEY456  
   Nothing else in the file should change (preserve indentation and line order).

2. Edit /home/user/app/config/credentials.toml so that  
   • password becomes "NewPass789"  
   • token    becomes "NEWTOKEN654"  
   Keep the quotation marks exactly as shown.

3. Create a plain-text log file at /home/user/rotation.log that records the four changes you made.  
   The log must contain **exactly four lines**, each line a single JSON object with no extra whitespace or trailing commas, in the precise order shown below:

{"file":"/home/user/app/config/settings.yml","field":"aws.access_key","old":"OLDACCESSKEY","new":"NEWACCESSKEY123"}  
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/settings.yml","field":"aws.secret_key","old":"OLDSECRETKEY","new":"NEWSECRETKEY456"}  
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/credentials.toml","field":"default.password","old":"OldPass123","new":"NewPass789"}  
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/credentials.toml","field":"default.token","old":"OLDTOKEN987","new":"NEWTOKEN654"}  

Do not add blank lines, spaces, or any other text.

Execution constraints:

• Finish everything using no more than three shell commands.  
• Do not create or leave temporary files outside /home/user.  
• You do not have sudo privileges.

The automated tests will deem the task successful only if  
a) the two configuration files reflect the new credential values and differ from their originals solely in those four scalar values, and  
b) /home/user/rotation.log exists and its content is byte-for-byte identical to the four JSON lines above.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1) /home/user/app/config/settings.yml   (permissions 644)
database:
  user: admin
  password: OldPass123
aws:
  access_key: OLDACCESSKEY
  secret_key: OLDSECRETKEY
  region: us-east-1

2) /home/user/app/config/credentials.toml   (permissions 644)
[default]
username = "admin"
password = "OldPass123"
token    = "OLDTOKEN987"

Expected final state after successful completion:

/home/user/app/config/settings.yml
database:
  user: admin
  password: OldPass123
aws:
  access_key: NEWACCESSKEY123
  secret_key: NEWSECRETKEY456
  region: us-east-1

/home/user/app/config/credentials.toml
[default]
username = "admin"
password = "NewPass789"
token    = "NEWTOKEN654"

/home/user/rotation.log   (permissions 644, exactly 4 lines)
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/settings.yml","field":"aws.access_key","old":"OLDACCESSKEY","new":"NEWACCESSKEY123"}
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/settings.yml","field":"aws.secret_key","old":"OLDSECRETKEY","new":"NEWSECRETKEY456"}
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/credentials.toml","field":"default.password","old":"OldPass123","new":"NewPass789"}
{"file":"/home/user/app/config/credentials.toml","field":"default.token","old":"OLDTOKEN987","new":"NEWTOKEN654"}

No other files should be modified or created.
